evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: data modelling standards and engineering best practices
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on establishing 'data modelling standards and engineering best practices' with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because no sector is specified and no financial/regulatory language appears; the role supports internal organisational analytics without named compliance requirements.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Science or Machine Learning teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: a rare greenfield opportunity where you'll be the first dedicated Analytics Engineer within the business
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as early because the JD explicitly states the candidate is 'the first dedicated Analytics Engineer within the business' with a mandate to build the analytics platform from scratch.
evidence.jd_authorship: first dedicated Analytics Engineer; brand-new analytics platform; semantic layer design; Databricks and/or Snowflake
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because specific tools (Databricks, Snowflake, Azure), the greenfield mission, and semantic layer design context tell the reader exactly what the role involves.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: modern cloud-based data platform that will support the organisation's next phase of growth
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the role's primary output is the analytics platform itself — serving the organisation's internal data consumers rather than a named business function.
evidence.autonomy_level: take ownership of platform architecture, data modelling standards, and engineering best practices
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as strategic because 'take ownership of platform architecture, data modelling standards, and engineering best practices' as the first Analytics Engineer signals full directional authority.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Data Science/ML collaboration is mentioned as advantageous but no AI tool usage by the candidate or AI infrastructure enablement is specified as a role expectation.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as absent because neither testing tools, data contracts, observability, nor data quality practices are mentioned anywhere in the posting.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as none because the JD is entirely framed in delivery and capability terms with no risk, compliance, or stakeholder-trust language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: rare greenfield opportunity where you'll be the first dedicated Analytics Engineer within the business
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as greenfield because the explicit mission is to build a brand-new analytics platform with the candidate as the first AE in the organisation.
